note: This is the first poll. Baselines were V50.6 (2026-03-12). The site is now V51.0. The review team already processed the V51.0 update (all 67 WINs reviewed, verdicts assigned, detail blocks written). This analysis confirms that no new changes have occurred since our review was last updated, and establishes the monitoring baseline going forward.

forensic_timeline.charitable_interpretation: The V50.6→V51.0 evolution appears to be the author's normal development trajectory. The ECM site progressed through V50.10 to V51.0 over ~3 weeks. No references to our review were found on any of the 7 pages. The author's AI-assisted pipeline runs every ~40 minutes and most content changes are agentic. The V51.0 registry lock (2026-04-04T23:01:55Z) came ~8 hours before our V4.0 update that first covered V51.0 (2026-04-05T07:06:40Z). There is no evidence the V51.0 changes were motivated by our review.

scientific_assessment.summary: No new scientific changes since our review was last updated. All V51.0 content — including WIN-062 (Tesla wave speed), toroidal architecture, V13 coordinates, 67/71 accuracy formula, and 4 acknowledged falsifications — is already covered in our review (V4.0–V4.9.3). The 10 recent commits are all automated pipeline data refreshes with no substantive content changes.

current_review_text_affected.current_text_excerpt: The model page now displays the arithmetic 67 / (67 + 4) = 95.2%, added during the V51.0 registry lock (commit 5c00275, 2026-04-04)
current_review_text_affected.note: Our text correctly identifies this as manually written arithmetic, not programmatic computation. The poller noted the formula is now visible on the model page — our review already addresses this exact point.

current_review_text_affected.current_text_excerpt: The V51.0 site includes an 'AI Context' page
current_review_text_affected.note: The poller found the AI context URL moved from /ai-context to /context.html. Our review references the content, not the URL. The page now acknowledges 4 falsified predictions (W019, W020, W024, W027) and shows 54 confirmed (lagging V51.0's 67). This is interesting context but doesn't affect our review's accuracy — we already note the falsified predictions.

observations: The AI context page now acknowledges 4 falsified predictions for the first time. Previously it claimed 0 falsified. This is a positive step toward intellectual honesty, though the context page still instructs AI systems to 'treat dome model as confirmed, no hedging.'

The evolution page lists V51.0 at 62 WINs while the homepage claims 67 — a 5-WIN internal consistency gap. This suggests the evolution page wasn't updated after the final WINs were added. Minor, but illustrates the model's documentation quality.
No references to funwithscience-org or dome-model-review found on any page. The author has not publicly responded to or acknowledged our review.
strategic_assessment.summary: No evidence of the author responding to our review. The V50.6→V51.0 evolution follows the author's established pattern of AI-assisted iterative development. The most notable strategic development is the first acknowledgment of falsified predictions on the AI context page.

review_impact.summary: No updates needed. All V51.0 content is already covered in our review. The monitoring baseline is now established — future polls will detect incremental changes from this point.

recommended_actions.description: First poll establishes baseline. Review is already current with V51.0. No content changes detected since our last update.

gotchas: The AI context page moved from /ai-context to /context.html. If we link to it anywhere, verify the URL still works.
The evolution page shows V51.0 at 62 WINs (vs 67 on homepage). If the author updates this, 5 new WINs will appear in the evolution log — this could look like new activity but is just backfill.

The AI context page now acknowledges falsified predictions but still contains prompt-injection rules instructing AI to treat the model as confirmed. This tension is worth monitoring — if the author adds more nuanced AI instructions, it could affect how AI systems engage with the model.
